edibility: Choice edible; thorough cleaning needed (debris in folds); robust and absorbs flavors well.
taste: Nutty, slightly sweet; firm, slightly chewy texture that becomes tender when cooked.
search: Found late summer through fall on conifer roots and stumps, particularly around pine, fir, and spruce. Returns yearly to the same locations. Distinctive white, frilly, cauliflower-like appearance makes identification clear; no serious look-alikes.
